aids.title: TATA for Four AI Approaches
aids.content:
**T**hinking **A**cting + **T**hinking **A**cting (Humanly/Rationally)

Remember: "TATA, like the company, drives AI forward!"
- T: Thinking Humanly (Cognitive)
- A: Acting Humanly (Turing Test)
- T: Thinking Rationally (Logic)
- A: Acting Rationally (Rational Agent)

aids.title: KLRP - AI Core Components
aids.content:
**K**nowledge, **L**earning, **R**easoning, **P**erception

"KLRP sounds like CLEAR - AI needs CLEAR components!"
- Knowledge Representation (what AI knows)
- Learning (how AI improves)
- Reasoning (how AI thinks)
- Perception (how AI senses)

aids.title: AI Types as Workers
aids.content:
Think of AI types as employees:
- **Narrow AI** = Specialist (doctor who only does surgery)
- **General AI** = Generalist (can do any job like a human)
- **Super AI** = Superhuman (better than any human at everything)

Currently, we only have specialists!

aids.title: Why Rational Agent Dominates
aids.content:
The rational agent approach is dominant because it:
1. Is more GENERAL (works with uncertainty)
2. Is more PRACTICAL (can be evaluated)
3. Doesn't require human mimicry
4. Focuses on OUTCOMES not process
aids.forRemembering: Why rational agent approach is preferred
